title: Visual Redstone Vanilla Plus
description: See what your redstone is doing! Vanilla-friendly - no mods needed.
body:
![A collage of Minecraft blocks that have been edited from how they appear in the base game to make it easier to whether they are being powered and other such properties.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/dfd9afee1cc5706dbc132704935e83bd68ac26ea.png)

## About
This resource pack makes it easier to see and understand redstone devices by adding visible mechanisms, lights and other indicators to various blocks while remaining as vanilla-looking as possible. 

Inspired by [Vanilla Tweaks](https://vanillatweaks.net/) and [Redstone Tweaks](https://modrinth.com/resourcepack/redstone-tweaks), and also uses some of those packs' model files and a handful of edited textures as a base for this pack's custom tweaks. No straight-up copies. 

I recommend the 'Unlit Redstone Ore', 'Groovy Levers' and 'Visual Honey Stages' Vanilla Tweaks to go alongside this pack, as they complement it perfectly. Place VRVP at the top of the resource pack list so it has priority over everything else that might overwrite it.

## Changed Blocks

- Cauldrons
- Composters
- Daylight Detectors
- Dispensers
- Droppers
- Hoppers
- Jukeboxes
- Leaves
- Note Blocks
- Observers
- Pistons and Sticky Pistons
- Repeater and Comparator undersides
- Scaffolding
- Target Blocks
- Tripwire

Check the Gallery images and descriptions to see how these blocks are changed.

## Credits

Made by batbrain55

Inspired by, and using .json files and a few edited textures from Redstone Tweaks: https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/texture-packs/redstone-tweaks

Inspired by, and using an edited version of the Pixel Consistent Tripwire from Vanilla Tweaks for older versions: https://vanillatweaks.net/

Base textures and the video game of Minecraft by Mojang
